title: Smart Spending Checkpoint
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you maintain financial discipline while still enjoying life experiences?
What strategies help you make smart spending decisions consistently?

stages.prompt: Before making a purchase, what should you ask yourself first?

options.label: Do I want this right now?
options.reflection: Asking only if you want something right now can lead to impulse purchases. It's better to consider if you actually need it.

options.label: Do I actually need this?
options.reflection: Perfect! Asking if you actually need an item helps distinguish between wants and needs, leading to smarter spending decisions.

options.label: Can I afford it?
options.reflection: While affordability is important, considering whether you need the item is more fundamental to smart spending.

options.label: Is this cheaper than other options?
options.reflection: Price comparison is useful, but the more important question is whether you need the item at all.

stages.prompt: How should you approach sales and discounts?

options.label: Only buy if you were planning to purchase anyway
options.reflection: Exactly! Taking advantage of sales on items you were already planning to buy is a smart financial strategy.

options.label: Buy items just because they're on sale
options.reflection: Buying items just because they're on sale can lead to unnecessary purchases. A good deal on something you don't need is still a bad purchase.

options.label: Act quickly before the sale ends
options.reflection: Rushing to buy during sales can lead to poor financial decisions. Take time to consider if you need the item.

options.label: Buy extra items to save more money
options.reflection: Buying extra items just to save more money can lead to overspending on things you don't need.

stages.prompt: What's the best way to handle peer pressure to spend?

options.label: Follow along to fit in with the group
options.reflection: Following others' spending habits to fit in can lead to financial stress and poor financial decisions.

options.label: Set and maintain your spending boundaries
options.reflection: Perfect! Setting and maintaining spending boundaries helps you make decisions based on your financial goals rather than peer pressure.

options.label: Spend more to show financial capability
options.reflection: Competing financially with others often leads to overspending and financial stress.

options.label: Avoid all social spending situations
options.reflection: While setting boundaries is important, avoiding all social spending situations might limit positive experiences.

stages.prompt: How should you handle lifestyle inflation as income increases?

options.label: Increase spending proportionally with income
options.reflection: Increasing spending proportionally with income can lead to lifestyle inflation, where you live paycheck to paycheck despite higher income.

options.label: Upgrade lifestyle immediately to enjoy more
options.reflection: Immediately upgrading your lifestyle when income increases is a classic case of lifestyle inflation, which can trap earners financially.

options.label: Keep spending exactly the same as before
options.reflection: While controlling spending is good, it's also reasonable to moderately increase spending on needs while prioritizing savings.

options.label: Increase savings and investments first
options.reflection: Exactly! Prioritizing savings and investments when income increases helps build wealth and prevents lifestyle inflation.

stages.prompt: What should you do when you feel emotional and want to spend?

options.label: Go shopping to feel better
options.reflection: Retail therapy often leads to regrettable purchases and doesn't address the underlying emotions causing the urge to spend.

options.label: Buy something small as a treat for yourself
options.reflection: Even small emotional purchases can develop into a habit that impacts your finances over time.

options.label: Wait 24 hours before making any purchase
options.reflection: Perfect! Waiting 24 hours before making a purchase helps separate emotional reactions from rational financial decisions.

options.label: Plan a big purchase to feel better
options.reflection: Big purchases driven by emotions often lead to significant financial regret and stress.
